description: Italian comune
description_wiki: Matera (Italian pronunciation: [maˈtɛːra], locally [maˈteːra] ; Materano: Matàrë [maˈtæːrə]) is a city and the capital of the Province of Matera in the region of Basilicata, in Southern Italy.

surface_forms: the dry valley of the Gravina River
description: river in Italy
description_wiki: The Gravina di Matera is a river in the Apulia and Basilicata regions of southern Italy.

surface_forms: neighborhoods built right into the side of the mountain
description: human settlement in Matera, Province of Matera, Basilicata, Italy
description_wiki: The Sassi di Matera are two districts (Sasso Caveoso and Sasso Barisano) of the Italian city of Matera, Basilicata, well-known for their ancient cave dwellings inhabited  since the Paleolithic period.

description: Italian painter, writer, politic and anti-fascist (1902-1975)
description_wiki: Carlo Levi (Italian pronunciation: [ˈkarlo ˈlɛːvi]) (29 November 1902 – 4 January 1975) was an Italian painter, writer, activist, independent leftist politician, and doctor.

surface_forms: Carlo Levi's famous memoir, Christ Stopped at Eboli
description: 1945 novel by Carlo Levi
description_wiki: Christ Stopped at Eboli (Italian: Cristo si è fermato a Eboli) is a memoir by Carlo Levi, published in 1945, giving an account of his exile from 1935–1936 to Grassano and Aliano, remote towns in Southern Italy, in the region of Lucania which is known today as Basilicata.
